issues.description: The customer listing and detail endpoints have no authentication or authorization guard. The comments state 'Authorization: Administrators can access this endpoint' and '@x-autobe-authorization-type null', but no guard is applied. This exposes all customer account data (email, ban status, profile info) to unauthenticated users.
issues.suggestion: Apply an AdministratorAuth guard to both endpoints if they are intended for admin use only. If public access is intentional, ensure sensitive fields like email and phone are not returned.

issues.description: The GET /reviews/:reviewId endpoint explicitly returns soft-deleted reviews and includes associated order item data (quantity, price, status) with no authentication required. The comment states 'Do not filter out deleted reviews - they may be needed for administrative review' and 'Client-side logic determines whether to display deleted reviews', which is insecure design for a public endpoint.
issues.suggestion: Either require authentication for accessing deleted reviews, or filter out deleted reviews for unauthenticated/non-admin requests. Sensitive order item data should not be exposed publicly.

issues.description: The seller cancellation request snapshots endpoint uses SellerAuth but the spec comment states 'Only administrators can access this endpoint' and 'Return 403 if user lacks administrator privileges'. There is a mismatch between the applied guard (SellerAuth) and the documented authorization requirement (administrator only). Sellers may be able to access snapshots they should not.
issues.suggestion: Clarify the intended authorization. If only administrators should access this, replace SellerAuth with AdministratorAuth. If sellers should only see their own snapshots, ensure the provider filters by seller.id.

issues.description: The provider queries seller profiles by profileId without verifying ownership against the authenticated customer. Any authenticated customer can retrieve any seller profile by guessing or knowing a UUID. While seller profiles may be semi-public, the endpoint is scoped under /customer/ suggesting it may expose more data than intended.
issues.suggestion: Verify whether seller profiles are intended to be public. If not, add an ownership or role check. If they are public, consider moving this endpoint out of the customer-scoped path.

issues.description: All controllers use console.log(error) for error handling. This is the standard AutoBE pattern but does not provide structured logging, log levels, or correlation IDs for production monitoring.
issues.suggestion: Consider integrating a structured logging library (e.g., Winston, Pino) with log levels and request correlation IDs for production deployments.

issues.description: No rate limiting is applied to any endpoint in this codebase. High-frequency endpoints like customer search and review listing could be abused for enumeration or DoS.
issues.suggestion: Apply rate limiting at the API gateway or using NestJS ThrottlerModule, especially on search/listing endpoints.

issues.description: The customer filter (customer_id) in the order item snapshots query is applied as an optional filter from the request body, not enforced from the authenticated customer's identity. A customer could pass any customer_id in the body and retrieve other customers' order item snapshots.
issues.suggestion: Always enforce `customer_id: props.customer.id` in the whereInput regardless of what is passed in the body, or ignore the body's customer_id field entirely and use only the authenticated customer's ID.

issues.description: The sortField value from the request body is used to construct an orderBy clause. While the switch statement limits valid values, the `typia.assert` on sortOrder could throw an unhandled exception if an invalid value is passed, potentially leaking internal error details.
issues.suggestion: Validate sortField and sortOrder against an explicit allowlist before using them in the query. Handle typia assertion errors gracefully.

issues.description: The function accepts the IShoppingMallProductImage.IUpdate body but does not actually update any image data — it only touches updated_at on the product. The image URLs or data from the body are never validated or stored, meaning the endpoint silently ignores the actual update payload. This could be a logic flaw allowing unintended state.
issues.suggestion: Ensure the image update body is actually processed and stored. If this is intentional (e.g., triggering a snapshot), document it clearly and validate that the body contains expected data.

issues.description: The refresh token flow reuses the same session_id when issuing new tokens. If a refresh token is stolen and used before the legitimate user, the attacker gets new valid tokens while the session remains active. There is no token rotation or invalidation of the old refresh token.
issues.suggestion: Implement refresh token rotation: invalidate the old refresh token upon use and issue a new session_id or store a token version/nonce in the session to detect reuse.

issues.description: Failed refresh token attempts (invalid token, expired session, banned/deleted account) are not logged. This makes it difficult to detect brute-force or token theft attacks.
issues.suggestion: Log authentication failures with relevant context (timestamp, IP if available) for security monitoring.

issues.description: No rate limiting is applied to the token refresh endpoint, which could be abused to enumerate valid sessions or perform denial-of-service on session validation.
issues.suggestion: Apply rate limiting to authentication endpoints at the infrastructure or middleware level.

issues.description: This public product listing endpoint has no authentication requirement, which is acceptable for a public catalog. However, there is no rate limiting or pagination cap, allowing large limit values that could cause performance issues.
issues.suggestion: Enforce a maximum page size (e.g., limit <= 100) to prevent resource exhaustion from large data requests.

agentEvaluations.summary: [sampled 30/266 files] [2 chunks, 14→13 issues] The codebase has several access control concerns. Most critically, the customer listing/detail endpoints expose PII (email, phone, ban status) without any authentication guard despite being documented as admin-only. The reviews endpoint exposes deleted reviews and order data publicly. There is also an authorization mismatch in the seller cancellation snapshots controller where the spec requires admin-only access but a seller guard is applied. Provider-level code generally implements ownership checks correctly (e.g., wishlist, order item snapshots). Infrastructure-level concerns (rate limiting, structured logging) are noted as suggestions per AutoBE conventions.

issues.description: Importing from '@prisma/sdk' instead of '@prisma/client'. '@prisma/sdk' is not a valid npm package - Prisma's client package is '@prisma/client'.
issues.suggestion: Change import { Prisma } from '@prisma/sdk' to import { Prisma } from '@prisma/client' in all files.

issues.description: Function name and logic mismatch: Function is named 'putShoppingMallCustomerProfile' but operates on seller profiles (shopping_mall_seller_profiles). Customer should not have seller profile access.
issues.suggestion: Either rename function to operate on customer profile (if such entity exists) or change the function to operate on correct entity (customer profile instead of seller profile).

issues.description: Function signature mismatch: Function accepts IShoppingMallProductImage.IUpdate but returns IShoppingMallProduct. This doesn't make logical sense - updating product images should return updated images or product with images.
issues.suggestion: Either change return type to IShoppingMallProductImage[] or IShoppingMallProductImage, or change function to actually update product images and return appropriate result.

issues.description: Missing validation for quantity: No check that quantity > 0 or that variant has sufficient inventory.
issues.suggestion: Add validation for quantity > 0 and check inventory availability before updating cart item quantity.

issues.description: Snapshot logic error: images_after is set to imagesBeforeString (same as before), not capturing actual changes to images.
issues.suggestion: Properly compute images_after based on actual image changes or remove images from snapshot if not being updated.

issues.description: Unused imports: ArrayUtil, PasswordUtil, toISOStringSafe, jwt, IEntity, IShoppingMallProductVariantOption are imported but never used in the function.
issues.suggestion: Remove unused imports to clean up code.

issues.description: Importing from non-existent package '@prisma/sdk'. The correct Prisma client import should be '@prisma/client'.
issues.suggestion: Change import { Prisma } from '@prisma/sdk'; to import { Prisma } from '@prisma/client';

issues.description: The code incorrectly references a table 'shopping_mall_administrator_promotion_request_of_sellers' with column 'shopping_mall_administrator_promotion_requests_id', but the earlier query uses 'shopping_mall_administrator_promotion_request_id'. This suggests inconsistent table/column naming.
issues.suggestion: Verify the actual table and column names in the Prisma schema and ensure consistency across queries.

issues.description: The code creates an inventory record to restore stock when a cancellation is approved, but it does not check if the variant exists or is deleted. It also doesn't update any aggregate inventory count.
issues.suggestion: Add validation for the product variant's existence and consider updating a stock quantity field on the variant if the schema includes one.

issues.description: The query uses `FindManyArgs` type for `select` fields, which is incorrect. The `select` should be a plain object, not a Prisma query argument type.
issues.suggestion: Change `satisfies Prisma.shopping_mall_administrator_request_of_customersFindManyArgs` to a proper select object, e.g., `select: { customer_id: true }`.

issues.description: The code does not validate that the shipment's `order_id` (if provided) matches the order of all items. It only checks if `order_id` is provided, but if not, it uses the first item's order. This could allow shipping items from different orders in one shipment if no `order_id` is given.
issues.suggestion: Ensure all order items belong to the same order even when `order_id` is not provided by checking consistency among `orderItems[0].shopping_mall_order_id`.

issues.description: The code validates that quantity_change is non-zero, but in a real inventory system, a zero change might be valid for audit purposes (e.g., a stock count adjustment).
issues.suggestion: Consider allowing zero quantity_change if the business requires audit records without stock movement.

issues.description: Multiple imports are declared but never used: `ArrayUtil`, `jwt`, `typia`, `toISOStringSafe`, `IEntity`, `IShoppingMallAdministrator`.
issues.suggestion: Remove unused imports to clean up the code.

issues.description: Multiple imports are declared but never used: `ArrayUtil`, `Prisma`, `jwt`, `typia`, `PasswordUtil`, `toISOStringSafe`, `IEntity`, `IShoppingMallCategory`, `IShoppingMallCustomer`, `IShoppingMallCustomerAddress`, `IShoppingMallOrder`, `IShoppingMallProduct`, `IShoppingMallProductVariantOption`, `IShoppingMallSeller`, `IShoppingMallSellerProfile`, `IShoppingMallCancellationRequestSnapshot`.
issues.suggestion: Remove unused imports to clean up the code.

issues.suggestion: Verify the ShoppingMallCancellationRequestTransformer.select() includes orderItem relation, or fetch it separately.

issues.description: Multiple imports are unused: IEntity, IShoppingMallCustomerAddress, IShoppingMallOrder, IShoppingMallSeller, IShoppingMallSellerProfile, ArrayUtil, jwt, typia, v4, PasswordUtil, toISOStringSafe.
issues.suggestion: Remove unused imports to clean up the code.

issues.description: Field name 'responseText' in body doesn't match snake_case convention used elsewhere (e.g., 'response_reason' in cancellation).
issues.suggestion: Consider using consistent naming convention (snake_case) for all body fields.

issues.description: No validation to ensure shipment is in 'shipped' status before marking as delivered. Only checks delivered_at is null.
issues.suggestion: Add status check: ensure shipment.status === 'shipped' before allowing delivery.

issues.description: Unused imports: IEntity, IShoppingMallCategory, IShoppingMallCustomer, IShoppingMallCustomerAddress, IShoppingMallOrder, IShoppingMallProduct, IShoppingMallProductVariant, IShoppingMallProductVariantOption, IShoppingMallSeller, IShoppingMallSellerProfile, ArrayUtil, Prisma, jwt, typia, v4, PasswordUtil, toISOStringSafe. These appear to be copy-pasted from a template and are not used in the function.
issues.suggestion: Remove all unused imports to clean up the code.

issues.description: The function uses `orderItem.updated_at` as the delivery timestamp for calculating the 7-day refund window. This is an unrealistic assumption - `updated_at` could be updated for many reasons, not just delivery. The requirements likely need a proper delivery timestamp field.
issues.suggestion: Check if there's a proper delivery timestamp field in the order item or shipment tables. If not, this is a design issue that should be addressed in the schema.

issues.description: The function creates a snapshot with `product_category_id: product.shopping_mall_category_id ?? v4()`. Using `v4()` as a fallback for a missing category ID creates invalid data - it generates a random UUID that doesn't correspond to any actual category.
issues.suggestion: Handle missing categories properly - either require products to have a category, use a default category ID, or allow null values if the schema supports it.

issues.description: The function assumes `seller.sellerProfile` always exists and throws a 500 error if not. This is an unrealistic assumption - seller profiles might not be created yet or might be deleted.
issues.suggestion: Handle missing seller profiles more gracefully - either validate earlier in the process or provide default values for shop information.

issues.description: The function parameter is named `body: IShoppingMallCheckout.ICreate` but the interface suggests it's for checkout, while the function creates an order. This naming might be confusing.
issues.suggestion: Consider renaming the parameter or interface to better reflect that it's creating an order from checkout data.

issues.description: The template comment at the end mentions 'No matching Collector/Transformer found for this operation' and suggests calling `getDatabaseSchemas`, but this is just leftover template text, not actual code.
issues.suggestion: Remove the template comment block at the end of the file since it's not needed in the final implementation.

issues.description: Importing non-existent interfaces: IShoppingMallCancellationRequestSnapshot, IShoppingMallCategory, IShoppingMallCustomerAddress, IShoppingMallProduct, IShoppingMallProductVariant, IShoppingMallProductVariantOption, IShoppingMallSeller, IShoppingMallSellerProfile. These are not used in the function and appear to be hallucinated imports.
issues.suggestion: Remove unused imports: IShoppingMallCancellationRequestSnapshot, IShoppingMallCategory, IShoppingMallCustomerAddress, IShoppingMallProduct, IShoppingMallProductVariant, IShoppingMallProductVariantOption, IShoppingMallSeller, IShoppingMallSellerProfile.

issues.description: Importing non-existent interface: IShoppingMallAdministrator. This is not used in the function and appears to be a hallucinated import.
issues.suggestion: Remove unused import: IShoppingMallAdministrator.

issues.description: The function uses `session.expired_at < now` to check if session has expired, but the session's `expired_at` field is being updated to `refreshExpires` later.
